mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value below 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals strengthening labor demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and generates positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield negative 5-day forward returns in over 65% of cases, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer spending strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with mean forward return below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index signals weakening manufacturing output, which reduces corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 70% of observations, invalidating the negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive when inflation crosses the threshold, with a t-statistic above 2.0 for positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation rather than final demand, which signals an impending economic slowdown and negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence predicts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, contradicting the inventory glut mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production combined with stable consumer prices indicates a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks coincide with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
